title: ilex subcrenata
intro: Ilex subcrenata, also known as Japanese holly or evergreen holly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae. It is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree native to East Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The leaves of Ilex subcrenata contain flavonoids and glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. Ilex subcrenata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties.
